grounding_quotes.quote_text:
In 
July 2021, the BDS-3 announced the service supporting 
a kinematic decimeter-level and a static centimeter-level 
real-time PPP when using its GEO B2b signal (PPP-B2b) 
in China and surrounding areas.
grounding_quotes.original_quote: the BDS-3 announced the service supporting a kinematic decimeter-level and a static centimeter-level real-time PPP when using its GEO B2b signal (PPP-B2b)

grounding_quotes.quote_text:
Vol.:(0123456789)
1 3
GPS Solutions (2023) 27:192 
https://doi.org/10.1007/s10291-023-01532-3
ORIGINAL ARTICLE
Evaluation of real‑time kinematic positioning performance 
of the BDS‑3 PPP service on B2b signal
Peida Wu1 · Yidong Lou1 · Weixing Zhang1 · Jan Dousa2 · Huizhong He4,5 · Junbing Chai3 · Yongzhong Ouyang4,5 · 
Zhenyi Zhang1 · Xu Zou1
Received: 6 March 2023 / Accepted: 10 August 2023 / Published online: 25 August 2023 
© The Author(s), under exclusive licence to Springer-Verlag GmbH Germany, part of Springer Nature 2023
Abstract
The BeiDou global navigation satellite system (GNSS) of China (BDS-3) had full operation capacity on July 31, 2020, and 
started providing free precise point positioning (PPP) service on B2b signals.
grounding_quotes.original_quote: evaluation of a real-time PPP performance exploiting the PPP-B2b corrections

experiment_epoch.note: Multiple experiments across different platforms; specific dates not clearly stated in accessible text

dcb_handling.note: Performance evaluation paper — uses PPP-B2b corrections as-is without detailed DCB analysis

datasets.correction_source: BDS-3 PPP-B2b real-time correction stream from B2b signal

grounding_quotes.quote_text:
We present a general evaluation 
of a real-time PPP performance exploiting the PPP-B2b corrections in various user situations represented with static sta-
tions, a car, an offshore vessel and an aircraft.
grounding_quotes.original_quote: static stations, a car, an offshore vessel and an aircraft

metrics.correction_quality: PPP-B2b corrections less stable and higher by factor ~2 for GPS compared to BDS-3 MEO

main_results.summary: PPP-B2b service is prospective for real-time kinematic positioning applications. BDS-3+GPS dual-system achieves 12.9 min average convergence for low-speed platforms. 95% positioning errors <20cm (H) and <35cm (V) after convergence.
key_findings: GPS PPP-B2b corrections ~2x less stable than BDS-3 MEO corrections
Convergence: 28.5 min (BDS only), 12.9 min (BDS+GPS) for low-speed objects
95% errors after convergence: <20cm (H), <35cm (V) for all experiments

grounding_quotes.quote_text:
An average convergence 
time of 28.5 and 12.9 min can be achieved with a standalone BDS-3 and BDS-3 + GPS solution for a low-speed object, such 
as a permanent station, a car, and a ship, in real time when using the PPP-B2b corrections.
grounding_quotes.original_quote: An average convergence time of 28.5 and 12.9 min can be achieved with a standalone BDS-3 and BDS-3+GPS solution

grounding_quotes.quote_text:
The 95% positioning errors after convergence are less than 
20 and 35 cm in horizontal and vertical directions for all the experiments.
grounding_quotes.original_quote: The 95% positioning errors after convergence are less than 20 and 35 cm in horizontal and vertical directions

novelty_audit.rationale: First comprehensive evaluation of PPP-B2b across multiple kinematic platforms (static, car, vessel, aircraft). Extends previous static-only PPP-B2b evaluations to real-time kinematic scenarios.

grounding_quotes.quote_text:
Although GNSS community has conducted 
many evaluations on the PPP-B2b products and its user performance, comprehensive evaluations of real-time PPP solutions 
using the BDS-3 PPP service at kinematic and highly kinematic platforms are still absent.
grounding_quotes.original_quote: comprehensive evaluations of real-time PPP solutions using the BDS-3 PPP service at kinematic and highly kinematic platforms are still absent

reproduction_blockers: Raw GNSS observation data from car/vessel/aircraft not publicly available
PPP-B2b correction stream archive not available
PPP processing software configuration not disclosed
No source code or processing scripts released
